name: sulis-execution
description: Work Package Executor — three-layer architecture (per v0.9.0) plus plan-mode-style Step 1.5 plan generation (v0.10.0+). v0.10.6 fixes a false-positive in _detect_branch_ci: the v0.10.4 substring-grep heuristic matched any Conventional Commits prefix anywhere in a workflow YAML — including paths-ignore filters. A real deploy-on-push-to-dev workflow with `paths-ignore: ['docs/**']` was misclassified as branch CI (because `docs/` matched the substring), causing wpx-pipeline to poll for 45 min waiting for check-runs that never appeared. Replaced with a structural parser that walks each .github/workflows/*.y[a]ml file looking specifically for `branches:` declarations (under on.push or on.pull_request), parsing inline lists and block lists. Explicitly rejects `branches-ignore`, `paths`, `paths-ignore`, `tags`, and comments. Two new regression tests pinned: paths-ignore must not register; branches-ignore must not register. v0.10.5 shipped the pytest test suite + two bug fixes (already-merged branch detection + multi-table INDEX parsing). v0.10.4 fixed emit_ok exit_code + no-branch-CI hang. v0.10.3 closed the auto-draft INDEX-registration gap. v0.10.2 fixed wpx-step12 dispatcher correctness. v0.10.1 fixed wpx-* tool discoverability. 43 tests across unit/ + integration/. Composes with CP-01..CP-05, AAF-01..AAF-09, EP-02/03/07, RGB-01..03. v0.10.7: wpx-pipeline's Step 10a health check now auto-detects the health path from --smoke-cmd's URL (e.g. `curl -sf https://staging/health` → /health), with --health-path flag for explicit override. Fixes the bug where APIs whose root returns 404 by design (health only at /health) produced spurious 10-minute hangs followed by health_status: unhealthy BLOCKERs. Backward-compatible: falls back to / when no URL is in the smoke command. Three regression tests pinned (49 tests total). v0.11.0: implements ADR-212 release-train / merge-queue (wpx-train tool). New per-batch path replaces the per-WP wpx-pipeline default; wpx-pipeline remains for hotfix / solo-ship via /sulis-execution:run-wp WP-X --force-single. wpx-train discovers eligibility from INDEX.md + origin branch list + per-WP CI status (no separate queue file per ADR-212 D6 amended); packs into batches up to 5 WPs; rebases each branch onto the previous rebased branch's HEAD; runs bundled-tip CI; sequential squash-merges; one deploy + health + smoke per batch. Failure modes: per-WP rebase conflict flips INDEX status to step-7-blocked + writes BLOCKER + train continues with the rest; bundled-tip CI fail flips all in-batch to step-7-blocked + writes train BLOCKER with file-overlap-heuristic culprit; post-merge deploy/health/smoke fail triggers full revert sequence (single wrapper commit on dev) + branch restoration with force-push guard (verifies origin/branch SHA equals rebased_to_sha before resetting to pre_train_sha). Step 11 security review stays per-WP, parallel, after batch deploy. New status enum values: step-7-complete (executor finished, queued for train), step-7-held (founder-paused), step-7-blocked (train failed for this WP). Polling + git/gh helpers extracted from wpx-pipeline into _wpxlib.py (Phase 0 refactor; no behaviour change to existing wpx-pipeline path). 101 tests pass (49 original + 52 new). Skill changes: /sulis-execution:run-wp default flips INDEX status (no auto-dispatch); /sulis-execution:run-all calls wpx-train run at batch boundaries; /sulis-execution:status surfaces eligible-count + trigger state; lifecycle.md documents the per-batch/per-WP split. Deferred to v0.12.0: primitive-driven batch_hint (cross-plugin SEA change); O(log N) bisect fallback to the file-overlap heuristic. v0.12.0: implements the Change Work Standard (CW-01..CW-08 published by SRD v1.22.0) — every piece of work is now bounded by a change branch + worktree, with the executor + train operating inside it. New sulis-change CLI tool with five subcommands: start (provision branch + worktree), adopt (retrofit in-flight work via stash + cherry-pick), finish (rebase + merge/PR + cleanup), list (enumerate active changes), status (one change's summary). Two-level worktree hierarchy — change worktree at the top, executor WP worktrees nested inside, branching off the change branch (not dev). wpx-pipeline and wpx-train gain a --base-branch parameter (default: dev) for backward compatibility; the calling session passes the change branch name when inside a change worktree. Skill markdown updated: run-wp Step 0 detects change context; run-all wpx-train invocation passes --base-branch; status surfaces the active change. Markdown-only update to sulis-concierge — the concierge picks a primitive + slug from founder intent, runs sulis-change start, then invokes downstream specialists inside the change worktree. References + READMEs document the two-level worktree hierarchy and the change-bounded lifecycle. 155 tests pass (101 prior + 54 new for sulis-change). Backward-compatible: when invoked outside a change worktree, --base-branch defaults to dev and behaviour is identical to v0.11.0. v0.13.0: ships the sulis-execution SDK at plugins/sulis-execution/sdk/ — a typed Python + TypeScript + MCP-server surface for the 10 CLI tools (38 operations). Schema source: sulis-execution.openapi.yaml (OpenAPI 3.1). Transport: Subprocess + JSON-on-stdout per agent-consumable-sdk-spec v0.2.0 Part 4.3 (primary, for the Python + TS clients); JSON-RPC over stdio / MCP per Part 4.2 (secondary, for the MCP server consumed by Claude Desktop / Cursor). Error model: outcome-category hierarchy (ProtocolError / ExpectedError / InternalError) with wpx-domain extensions; load-bearing rule that `outcome: blocker` is a normal result not an exception. Python package `sulis-execution` (Pydantic v2; sync + async clients); TypeScript package `@sulis-ai/execution` (typed interfaces; async-only). MCP server `sulis-execution-mcp` reads the OpenAPI spec at startup and registers each operation as an MCP tool with the operation's description as the LLM-facing prompt. Documentation per Diátaxis quadrants (~26 doc files): tutorials per language + per transport; how-to guides for error handling, configuration, mock-for-testing; reference catalogues for operations, errors, transport bindings, configuration, MCP tools; explanation essays for mental model, error categories, blocker-not-exception, retry strategy, field case conventions, versioning policy; recipes (run-train-after-executors, ship-change-end-to-end). Rubric validation pass produces PASS-WITH-RATIONALE verdict (0 MUST failures; 6 SHOULD-failures deferred with rationale; 99 PASS; 17 N/A; 2 UNVERIFIABLE — see sdk/SDK-VALIDATION-REPORT.md). Total test count across the plugin: 206 tests (155 CLI + 25 Python SDK + 17 MCP + 9 TypeScript). All existing tests pass; zero regressions. The underlying CLI tools (wpx-pipeline, wpx-train, sulis-change, etc.) are unchanged — the SDK wraps them as the public surface. v0.14.0: SDK v0.2.0 naming sweep — every method name is now self-describing without insider knowledge. Resource renames: step12 → lifecycle, wp → work_package. Method renames (9): step12.wrap → lifecycle.complete, wp.read_frontmatter → work_package.read_metadata, index.add_wp → index.add, index.propagate_blocked → index.mark_downstream_blocked, index.sync_auto_drafts → index.register_pending_drafts, findings.auto_draft_wp → findings.draft_remediation, journal.seed_plan → journal.create_plan, journal.mark_plan_item → journal.update_plan_item, journal.record_postdeploy → journal.record_security_verdict. Result type + MCP tool names renamed in parallel. The OpenAPI spec gains an x-cli-subcommand extension on the 9 renamed operations so the SDK method dispatches to the correct underlying CLI subcommand (e.g. lifecycle.complete → wpx-step12 wrap). Migration guide at sdk/docs/migrations/v0.1-to-v0.2.md with per-rename before/after snippets + sed expressions. Underlying CLI binaries + subcommands unchanged. 209 tests pass (155 wpx + 25 Python SDK + 9 TS + 20 MCP). Triggered by peer-SDK test session feedback that surfaced naming-coherence as a real first-time-user obstacle; rubric v0.2.0 added 5 checks (4.01a, 5.01a, 10.A.04a, 12.09, 12.10) catching this class. v0.15.0: ships .mcp.json at the plugin root so the sulis-execution-mcp MCP server auto-loads whenever the plugin is enabled. Any Claude session that loads sulis-execution gets the 38 typed tools natively (addressable as `mcp__sulis-execution-mcp__<tool_name>`); no manual claude_desktop_config.json editing required. Per Claude Code's plugin manifest support for mcpServers, servers start when the plugin is enabled and connect at session startup; /reload-plugins reconnects them mid-session. Env vars (SULIS_EXECUTION_REPO_ROOT, SULIS_EXECUTION_PROJECT, WPX_DIR) have sensible defaults derived from CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT; can be overridden per-shell. Prerequisite: the sulis-execution-mcp Python package must be installed (pip install plugins/sulis-execution/sdk/mcp-server/) — the .mcp.json references it as a console script. v0.15.1: three real bug fixes surfaced by a live executor session against the platform repo. (1) wpx-index flip-status argparse `choices=` list was missing step-7-complete / step-7-held / step-7-blocked — added in v0.11.0 to the SDK + train eligibility logic but never propagated to the CLI's argparse. SDK declared these statuses valid, train evaluated on them, but the CLI rejected them. Three new regression tests pin the contract. (2) wpx-train's rebase_branch_in_clone + restore_branch_to_pre_train used `git fetch origin <branch>` against a clone created by `gh repo clone --depth 100` (single-branch shallow). Under single-branch mode, that fetch updates FETCH_HEAD but does NOT create refs/remotes/origin/<branch> — the subsequent `git checkout -B <branch> origin/<branch>` failed. Fixed by using explicit refspec form: `git fetch origin <branch>:refs/remotes/origin/<branch>`. (3) The same path's `git push --force-with-lease` interacted badly with the refspec-form fetch's freshly-created tracking ref, producing `stale info` errors. Fixed by capturing pre-rebase SHA from `git rev-parse origin/<branch>` and using explicit `--force-with-lease=<branch>:<pre_rebase_sha>` form — same safety guarantee, deterministic comparison. wpx-pipeline path (worktree-based, not clone-based) is unaffected. 158 tests pass. v0.15.3: detect patches-already-applied at start of rebase. Live executor session hit this when running train against WPs whose content had been squash-merged then reverted on dev — `git rebase` saw the patches as already-applied (via patch-id match against the still-present reverted squash commits), silently dropped them, branch HEAD ended at base HEAD, GitHub /merges returned 204, train failed opaquely. Fix: before rebase, run `git cherry origin/<base> origin/<feature>` and check for `-` prefix lines. If detected, raise new PatchesAlreadyAppliedError (RuntimeError subclass; existing handling still catches via `except RuntimeError`); wpx-train's rebase loop catches it specifically and writes a per-WP BLOCKER with the manual revert-of-revert recovery command. Also fixes a latent bug — rebase_branch_in_clone hardcoded 'dev' for the second fetch even though wpx-train --base-branch supports change branches. Now accepts base_branch param (defaults 'dev'). 9 new tests pin: cherry parsing, exception structure, RuntimeError-subclass guarantee, pre-rebase detection (rebase MUST NOT execute), base_branch parameter flow. 169 wpx tests pass. (1) DEPLOY_DEFAULT_CAP was hard-coded to 30 min and not exposed as a CLI flag. A real platform-repo deploy took ~35 min, hit the cap, triggered ADR-212 full revert even though the deploy actually succeeded. Fix: raised default to 60 min + added `--deploy-cap` flag to both wpx-train and wpx-pipeline (seconds; overrides default). (2) restore_branch_with_guard failed to restore branches when GitHub's delete-branch-on-merge had auto-deleted them. The function did `git fetch origin <branch>` first; fetch returned non-zero because origin had no such ref; restore was skipped → user's feature branches stayed deleted from origin. Fix: probe `git ls-remote --heads origin <branch>` first. If branch is missing on origin (auto-deleted), push pre_train_sha as a fresh branch create (no force needed — nothing to clobber). If present, existing guard logic applies. Two new regression tests cover both auto-deleted-branch sub-paths (success + push-fails). 160 wpx tests pass. v0.16.0: adds Step 6.5 to the executor lifecycle — per-WP code-review gate between Step 6 (lint pass) and Step 7 (commit + push). Mandatory; every finding from /code-review must be addressed before step-7-complete. Three valid resolution paths per finding: inline fix (loop /code-review until zero findings; budget 3 iterations), auto-drafted remediation WP via findings.draft_remediation for findings out-of-scope, or founder-flagged exception via BLOCKER (trigger=code-review-exception). Binary verdict: addressed (proceed to Step 7) / not-addressed (BLOCKER + step-7-blocked + executor exits). Anti-pattern explicit: suppressing findings (silencing patterns, deleting from report, marking step complete without addressing) is FORBIDDEN. Step 6.5 catches defects per-WP; companion Step 10.5 (train-batch review; ships in next commit) catches cross-WP composition issues. Existing tools used; no new CLI subcommands needed. executor.md gains ~120-line Step 6.5 detailed section; lifecycle.md gains 6.5 row + Step 6.5 narrative. 169 wpx tests pass. v0.16.1: documents the Step 10.5 design (train-batch code-review gate) in lifecycle.md as DEFERRED, with workflow + rationale captured for the follow-on session that ships the wpx-train split. Step 10.5 catches cross-WP composition issues (N+1 queries across sibling WPs, integration regressions) that no per-WP gate can see; companion to the v0.16.0 Step 6.5 per-WP gate. Implementation deferred because it requires splitting wpx-train's cmd_run into three commands (rebase-and-ci / continue-merge-and-deploy / abort-pre-merge) with state-file persistence and real-fixture integration testing; not appropriate for a rushed final commit. v0.16.2: bug fix — short WP-name resolution in dependency check. INDEX.md's `Depends On` / `Blocks` columns commonly use short names (e.g. `PERMS, MANIFEST, MCP-STUB`) for visual brevity, but `find_eligible_branches` was comparing them against full WP IDs (`WP-S2-PERMS, WP-S2-MANIFEST, WP-S2-MCP-STUB`). Inequality → dep treated as not-merged → WP marked ineligible even when deps are done. Founder hit this with WP-S2-LOADER-CORE blocked by 3 short-name deps that were all on dev. Fix: new `_normalise_wp_reference(ref, known_wps)` helper in `_wpxlib.py`; `parse_index_md` runs a normalisation pass after building WPRows. Unique suffix match → resolve to full ID; ambiguous → ValueError naming candidates; unknown → preserved + optional warning. `cmd_doctor` calls `parse_index_md(verbose_normalisations=True)` so founders see when their INDEX has shorthand. Backward compatible: full IDs pass through unchanged; tests pin all 4 cases (full passthrough, unique suffix match, ambiguous-raises, unknown-preserved) plus the Blocks-column equivalent. 13 new tests; 182 wpx tests pass. v0.17.0a (state machine foundation; Phase 1.1 of the Resumable Train rollout): introduces the in-flight train state machine — 10 named phases (pending / rebasing / ci_running / merging / deploying / verifying / success / failed / paused / aborted), persisted as JSON at `.architecture/{project}/train-runs/{train_id}.state.json` alongside the existing .yaml record (YAML remains the historical archive; JSON enables trivial round-trip without pyyaml). New helpers in `_wpxlib.py`: init_train_state, read_train_state, write_train_state (atomic via tmp+rename), update_train_phase (closes previous phase in history; opens new; sets pause_reason+recovery_hint when transitioning to paused/failed; sets completed_at on terminal phases; idempotent for same-phase calls), update_wp_phase_outcome (per-WP per-phase outcome dict; accumulates across phases), cleanup_train_state (terminal-phase cleanup), train_state_path + train_lock_path (path helpers), TrainLock (fcntl flock context manager; second concurrent acquisition raises RuntimeError naming the existing holder's PID; cleanup on exit + exception), PHASES + TERMINAL_PHASES (frozenset {success,failed,aborted}) constants. Backward compatible — existing write_train_run_record unchanged. cmd_run integration happens in Phase 3.1 (wpx-train resume) when the per-phase factoring lands together with the resume logic; this commit ships the foundation primitives with comprehensive tests. 18 new tests pin: init shape, round-trip, missing/corrupt handling, phase advance with history, terminal completed_at, paused reason+hint, unknown-phase rejection, idempotent same-phase, per-WP outcome targeting+accumulation+unknown-WP rejection, flock acquire/release/exception-cleanup/concurrent-collision, cleanup idempotency, PHASES constants. 200 wpx tests pass. v0.17.0 (Phase 1 of the Resumable Train rollout, complete): Phase 1.1's state machine foundation (above) plus the new `wpx-train inspect` subcommand. Read-only inspection of train state: with --train-id renders the train's state snapshot (phase, phase_history, per-WP outcomes, pause_reason+recovery_hint when present) in plain English by default or JSON with --json; without --train-id lists recent trains (most-recent first; mix of in-flight + terminal). New helpers in `_wpxlib.py`: render_train_state_plain_english (FE-06 compliant — phase-by-phase descriptions; surfaces pause_reason and 'What to do' line for paused state; bundle table with per-WP outcomes; phase history when present), list_train_runs (enumerates state.json + yaml records; in-flight wins over historical; sorted by started_at descending). SDK additions: new train.inspect operation (OpenAPI spec + Python + TypeScript + auto-registered MCP tool); new schemas TrainInspectRequest, TrainInspectResult, TrainStateSnapshot, TrainRunListing, TrainPhaseHistoryEntry, TrainBundleEntry-with-outcomes. SDK packages bumped 0.2.0 → 0.2.1 (purely additive — 1 new operation; v0.3.0 reserved for streaming roadmap). MCP server tool count 38 → 39. cmd_run state-machine integration deferred to Phase 3.1 (resume) when the per-phase factoring lands; Phase 1 ships observability without changing run behaviour. 23 new tests (rendering for all 10 phases; pause+hint surfacing; bundle outcomes; phase history; listing in-flight vs historical; ordering; preference for in-flight over yaml). 223 wpx tests + 25 Python SDK + 20 MCP + 9 TS = 277 tests pass. v0.18.0 (Phase 2 of the Resumable Train rollout): the founder no longer waits + non-fatal failures park as paused. Two changes compose: (1) Phase 2.1's optimistic eligibility — branch CI is informational, not gating; bundled-tip CI is the real gate; --strict-ci preserves v0.11.0 behaviour. (2) Phase 2.2's paused-state recovery — bundled-tip CI red/timeout and deploy poll timeout transition to phase=paused with pause_reason + recovery_hint instead of running the ADR-212 revert. Founders inspect via `wpx-train inspect <id>`, fix the underlying issue, then (when Phase 3.1 lands in v0.19.0) resume. Post-merge fatal failures (deploy explicitly failed, health unhealthy, smoke FAIL) continue to route through _handle_post_merge_failure (revert + step-7-blocked + failed terminal state) — production safety preserved. cmd_run now writes initial state YAML at start (phase=pending) + checkpoints at every phase boundary (pending→rebasing→ci_running→merging→deploying→verifying→success) + cleans up the state file on terminal phases. Full per-phase factoring (so cmd_resume can re-enter at any phase) ships in Phase 3.1. SDK packages stay at 0.2.1 (no new operations). New _pause_train_state helper alongside _handle_post_merge_failure; _handle_post_merge_failure also flips to phase=failed before cleanup. New ADR-212 amendment at docs/research/adr-212-eligibility-amendment.md. 7 new tests + 4 new eligibility tests + existing 220 = 234 wpx tests pass. v0.19.0a (Phase 3.1 of the Resumable Train rollout): new `wpx-train resume <train_id>` subcommand. Pragmatic implementation — pre-merge paused trains (rebasing/ci_running/paused-with-no-merging-in-history) are resumed via re-fire: state is marked aborted (audit trail preserved), WPs are restored to step-7-complete (in case prior failure handler flipped them), cmd_run is re-invoked with the args saved in state. The founder workflow becomes: paused → fix underlying issue → `wpx-train resume <id>` → train re-fires with same args. Post-merge paused phases (merging/deploying/verifying) emit a clear "use abort or manually flip merged WPs" error — full post-merge resume defers to a future release when real need surfaces. Terminal phases (success/failed/aborted) error with "nothing to resume". Lock via TrainLock prevents concurrent resume of the same train. SDK adds train.resume operation (Python + TypeScript + auto-registered MCP tool) with optional override params for deploy_workflow/staging_url/etc. SDK packages bump 0.2.1 → 0.2.2 (additive). 16 new tests pin: pre-merge phase recognition, post-merge phase recognition, args round-trip, bundle preservation, phase-by-phase decision tree (param-tested across all 10 named phases), paused-as-pre-merge vs paused-as-post-merge detection via phase_history, audit-trail preservation. 250 wpx tests pass. Phase 3.1 ships at v0.19.0a; v0.19.0 ships when 3.2 (abort) + 3.3 (retry-wp/skip-wp) land in subsequent commits. v0.19.0 (Resumable Train rollout complete): adds `wpx-train abort <id>` + `wpx-train skip-wp` + `wpx-train retry-wp`. **abort**: phase-aware; pre-merge restores branches + flips WPs to step-7-held; post-merge runs ADR-212 revert + flips to step-7-blocked + writes train BLOCKER. **skip-wp**: drops one WP from a paused train's bundle (flipped to step-7-held); founder runs resume next. **retry-wp**: clears one WP's per-phase outcomes (restored to step-7-complete); founder runs resume next. All three lock via TrainLock; all three preserve audit trail (state=aborted before cleanup; .yaml record retained). SDK adds 3 new operations (train.abort, train.skipWp, train.retryWp); 3 new result types (TrainAbortResult, TrainSkipWpResult, TrainRetryWpResult). SDK packages bump 0.2.2 → 0.2.3. MCP tool count 40 → 43. The full Resumable Train workflow is now live: optimistic eligibility (v0.18.0) → fire without waiting → paused-state recovery (v0.18.0) → no auto-revert on transient failures → inspect (v0.17.0) → understand what's wrong → resume (v0.19.0a) → re-fire after fix → abort / skip-wp / retry-wp (v0.19.0) → surgical recovery. 250 wpx tests still pass; no new tests for 3.2/3.3 in this commit — they're shippable on the existing helpers' test coverage; integration tests defer to the deferred per-phase cmd_run factoring.
